1→# Andes helper size optimization plan
     2→
     3→Audit of `andes_*.s` (top-level, not rv32e) for size-reducing edits.
     4→Execute one item per commit. After each: `make clean && make && make size-all`
     5→and run the mapped `make test-*` targets. All must PASS before committing.
     6→
     7→Patterns mirror prior commits on `s_*.s`:
     8→- c40cb6ed9 dead `mul`/`mulhu` removal
     9→- b672cb2f9 hoist duplicate `li` constants
    10→- 026bf3e50 `li 0; sw` -> `sw x0`
    11→- f21da4f44 swap `__adddf3` args to kill `mv` pair
    12→
    13→---
    14→
    15→## Tier A - trivial, mechanical (start here)
    16→
    17→### [A1] andes_0.s - fold adjacent `addi sp, sp, -16` pairs
    18→- Two pairs in qzero prologue+epilogue (~lines 219-220, matching epilogue)
    19→- Two pairs in pzero prologue+epilogue (~lines 235-236, matching epilogue)
    20→- Each pair collapses to single `addi sp, sp, -32` / `+32`
    21→- Saves ~4 instructions / 16 bytes
    22→- Verify: `make size-all` (no test target covers j0/y0)
    23→
    24→### [A2] andes_1.s - same as A1
    25→- Same pattern in qone/pone
    26→- Saves ~4 instructions / 16 bytes
    27→- Verify: `make size-all`
    28→
    29→### [A3] andes_n.s - fold adjacent sp adjusts (investigate first)
    30→- Lines ~53-54 and ~101-102 have two adjacent `addi sp, sp, 16`
    31→- Line 102 source comment says `#8` but instruction is `16`. Check git blame
    32→  to confirm whether the comment is stale or there is a latent bug. If bug,
    33→  fix it as separate commit before folding.
    34→- After confirmation, collapse adjacent pairs
    35→- Saves ~2 instructions / 8 bytes
    36→- Verify: `make size-all`
    37→
    38→### [A4] andes_fpexsqrt.s - remove 3 dead `mul` instructions
    39→- Lines 48-49: `mulhu x15, x13, x9; mul x14, x13, x9` - x14 killed before read
    40→- Lines 51-52: `mulhu x15, x9, x18; mul x14, x9, x18` - x14 killed before read
    41→- Lines 53-54: `mulhu x11, x15, x10; mul x10, x15, x10` - x10 overwritten
    42→- Verify each dead-ness by 10-line window read before editing
    43→- Saves ~3 instructions / 12 bytes in a 71-line file (largest relative win)
    44→- Verify: `make test-sqrtf`
    45→
    46→---
    47→
    48→## Tier B - dead mul removals + li hoists (one commit per file)
    49→
    50→### [B1] andes_fpexatan.s
    51→- Dead muls at ~145-146, ~212-213, ~228-229, ~240-241 (verify each)
    52→- Duplicate `li x15, 2147483648` at ~86, 155, 270 - hoist if liveness allows
    53→- Saves ~4-7 instructions
    54→- Verify: `make test-atanf test-asinf test-acosf`
    55→
    56→### [B2] andes_dpexsqrt.s
    57→- Dead muls in even/odd exponent arms (lines 18-20, 32-34)
    58→- Duplicate `li x9, 65535` at lines 55, 97, 116, 135 - hoist if x9 is free
    59→  across the span
    60→- Saves ~4-7 instructions
    61→- Verify: `make test-sqrt`
    62→
    63→### [B3] andes_fpexlog.s
    64→- Dead muls at ~162-166, ~174-175 (verify each)
    65→- Duplicate `li x21, 2147483648` at ~71, 82, 211, 261 - hoist candidate
    66→- Epilogue splits into 32+16-byte pops; combine if safe
    67→- Saves ~4-8 instructions
    68→- Verify: `make test-logf test-log10f test-powf`
    69→
    70→### [B4] andes_dpexatan.s
    71→- Dead mul at ~175-178
    72→- Duplicate `li ..., 2147483648` loads - hoist candidates
    73→- Saves ~2-5 instructions
    74→- Verify: `make test-atan test-asin test-acos`
    75→
    76→### [B5] andes_fpexexp.s
    77→- Dead mul at ~75-78 (`mul x10, x18, x10` then `mv x10, x11`)
    78→- Saves ~1 instruction
    79→- Verify: `make test-expf test-coshf test-sinhf test-tanhf test-powf`
    80→
    81→---
    82→
    83→## Tier C - scaffolding factoring (higher risk, do last)
    84→
    85→### [C1] andes_dpexlog.s - factor duplicate `__clzsi2` scaffolds
    86→- Two identical 14-line push/call/pop blocks around __clzsi2 (lines ~119-133
    87→  and ~207-221)
    88→- Collapse into a single in-file helper label + two calls
    89→- Saves ~10-12 instructions if done cleanly
    90→- Verify: `make test-log test-log10 test-pow`
    91→
    92→### [C2] andes_fpexlog.s - factor duplicate `__clzsi2` scaffolds
    93→- Same pattern as C1 applied to fpexlog
    94→- Saves ~10 instructions
    95→- Verify: `make test-logf test-log10f test-powf`
    96→
    97→### [C3] andes_dpexexp.s - factor duplicate `__gf_sigmax` call blocks
    98→- Two ~24-line save+call+restore sequences differing only in table address
    99→  (expcoo vs expcon), lines ~129-152 and ~154-176
   100→- Refactor to load table pointer before a shared `call`/`tail` block
   101→- Saves ~18-20 instructions - largest single-file win
   102→- Verify: `make test-exp test-cosh test-sinh test-tanh test-pow`
   103→
   104→---
   105→
   106→## Skipped (not worth it)
   107→
   108→- andes_dpreduct.s / andes_fpreduct.s - carry-chain code already minimal for
   109→  ADC-less RISC-V
   110→- andes_r.s / andes_r4.s - dispatcher code already tight
   111→- andes_up4.s .. andes_up8.s - each chains to the next; tightest possible
   112→- andes_p4.s .. andes_p8ac.s - prologue/epilogue minimal
   113→- Structural duplication of div/remu loops in dpexsqrt / fpexatan / dpexlog -
   114→  large potential, high correctness risk, defer indefinitely
   115→
   116→---
   117→
   118→## Verification checklist (per commit)
   119→
   120→1. Read 10-line window around each edit to confirm register liveness.
   121→2. `make clean && make` - must build cleanly.
   122→3. `make size-all > /tmp/after.txt` and diff vs baseline to confirm shrink.
   123→4. `make test-<f>` for every mapped function - every one must `PASS`.
   124→5. Commit via `/commit` (commit-optimizer skill) to capture size delta.
   125→6. If any test FAILs: revert, re-read, do not batch-fix.
   126→
   127→## Baseline capture (do once before starting)
   128→
   129→```
   130→make clean && make
   131→make size-all > /tmp/andes-size-baseline.txt
   132→```

file.content:
# Andes helper size optimization plan

Audit of `andes_*.s` (top-level, not rv32e) for size-reducing edits.
Execute one item per commit. After each: `make clean && make && make size-all`
and run the mapped `make test-*` targets. All must PASS before committing.

Patterns mirror prior commits on `s_*.s`:
- c40cb6ed9 dead `mul`/`mulhu` removal
- b672cb2f9 hoist duplicate `li` constants
- 026bf3e50 `li 0; sw` -> `sw x0`
- f21da4f44 swap `__adddf3` args to kill `mv` pair

---

## Tier A - trivial, mechanical (start here)

### [A1] andes_0.s - fold adjacent `addi sp, sp, -16` pairs
- Two pairs in qzero prologue+epilogue (~lines 219-220, matching epilogue)
- Two pairs in pzero prologue+epilogue (~lines 235-236, matching epilogue)
- Each pair collapses to single `addi sp, sp, -32` / `+32`
- Saves ~4 instructions / 16 bytes
- Verify: `make size-all` (no test target covers j0/y0)

### [A2] andes_1.s - same as A1
- Same pattern in qone/pone
- Saves ~4 instructions / 16 bytes
- Verify: `make size-all`

### [A3] andes_n.s - fold adjacent sp adjusts (investigate first)
- Lines ~53-54 and ~101-102 have two adjacent `addi sp, sp, 16`
- Line 102 source comment says `#8` but instruction is `16`. Check git blame
  to confirm whether the comment is stale or there is a latent bug. If bug,
  fix it as separate commit before folding.
- After confirmation, collapse adjacent pairs
- Saves ~2 instructions / 8 bytes
- Verify: `make size-all`

### [A4] andes_fpexsqrt.s - remove 3 dead `mul` instructions
- Lines 48-49: `mulhu x15, x13, x9; mul x14, x13, x9` - x14 killed before read
- Lines 51-52: `mulhu x15, x9, x18; mul x14, x9, x18` - x14 killed before read
- Lines 53-54: `mulhu x11, x15, x10; mul x10, x15, x10` - x10 overwritten
- Verify each dead-ness by 10-line window read before editing
- Saves ~3 instructions / 12 bytes in a 71-line file (largest relative win)
- Verify: `make test-sqrtf`

---

## Tier B - dead mul removals + li hoists (one commit per file)

### [B1] andes_fpexatan.s
- Dead muls at ~145-146, ~212-213, ~228-229, ~240-241 (verify each)
- Duplicate `li x15, 2147483648` at ~86, 155, 270 - hoist if liveness allows
- Saves ~4-7 instructions
- Verify: `make test-atanf test-asinf test-acosf`

### [B2] andes_dpexsqrt.s
- Dead muls in even/odd exponent arms (lines 18-20, 32-34)
- Duplicate `li x9, 65535` at lines 55, 97, 116, 135 - hoist if x9 is free
  across the span
- Saves ~4-7 instructions
- Verify: `make test-sqrt`

### [B3] andes_fpexlog.s
- Dead muls at ~162-166, ~174-175 (verify each)
- Duplicate `li x21, 2147483648` at ~71, 82, 211, 261 - hoist candidate
- Epilogue splits into 32+16-byte pops; combine if safe
- Saves ~4-8 instructions
- Verify: `make test-logf test-log10f test-powf`

### [B4] andes_dpexatan.s
- Dead mul at ~175-178
- Duplicate `li ..., 2147483648` loads - hoist candidates
- Saves ~2-5 instructions
- Verify: `make test-atan test-asin test-acos`

### [B5] andes_fpexexp.s
- Dead mul at ~75-78 (`mul x10, x18, x10` then `mv x10, x11`)
- Saves ~1 instruction
- Verify: `make test-expf test-coshf test-sinhf test-tanhf test-powf`

---

## Tier C - scaffolding factoring (higher risk, do last)

### [C1] andes_dpexlog.s - factor duplicate `__clzsi2` scaffolds
- Two identical 14-line push/call/pop blocks around __clzsi2 (lines ~119-133
  and ~207-221)
- Collapse into a single in-file helper label + two calls
- Saves ~10-12 instructions if done cleanly
- Verify: `make test-log test-log10 test-pow`

### [C2] andes_fpexlog.s - factor duplicate `__clzsi2` scaffolds
- Same pattern as C1 applied to fpexlog
- Saves ~10 instructions
- Verify: `make test-logf test-log10f test-powf`

### [C3] andes_dpexexp.s - factor duplicate `__gf_sigmax` call blocks
- Two ~24-line save+call+restore sequences differing only in table address
  (expcoo vs expcon), lines ~129-152 and ~154-176
- Refactor to load table pointer before a shared `call`/`tail` block
- Saves ~18-20 instructions - largest single-file win
- Verify: `make test-exp test-cosh test-sinh test-tanh test-pow`

---

## Skipped (not worth it)

- andes_dpreduct.s / andes_fpreduct.s - carry-chain code already minimal for
  ADC-less RISC-V
- andes_r.s / andes_r4.s - dispatcher code already tight
- andes_up4.s .. andes_up8.s - each chains to the next; tightest possible
- andes_p4.s .. andes_p8ac.s - prologue/epilogue minimal
- Structural duplication of div/remu loops in dpexsqrt / fpexatan / dpexlog -
  large potential, high correctness risk, defer indefinitely

---

## Verification checklist (per commit)

1. Read 10-line window around each edit to confirm register liveness.
2. `make clean && make` - must build cleanly.
3. `make size-all > /tmp/after.txt` and diff vs baseline to confirm shrink.
4. `make test-<f>` for every mapped function - every one must `PASS`.
5. Commit via `/commit` (commit-optimizer skill) to capture size delta.
6. If any test FAILs: revert, re-read, do not batch-fix.

## Baseline capture (do once before starting)

```
make clean && make
make size-all > /tmp/andes-size-baseline.txt
```

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/nick/.claude/skills/commit-optimizer

# Commit Optimizer Skill

Creates well-documented commit messages for optimization work with automatic benchmarking.

## Usage

```
/commit [problem="<description>"] [approach="<description>"]
```

## How It Works

1. **Validates optimization context** — checks that you're in an optimization commit (staged changes present)
2. **Detects what changed** — examines git diff to understand which functions/files were modified
3. **Runs benchmarks** — executes the project's measurement tools (Makefile, commit-size script, or QEMU tests) to capture before/after metrics
4. **Builds commit message** — combines problem statement, approach, and measured results into a structured message

## Commit Message Format

The skill generates messages in this format:

```
optimize: <one-line summary>

Problem:
<explanation of what was slow/large and why it mattered>

Approach:
<description of the optimization technique used>

Result:
<measured improvement with specific metrics>
```

## Examples

**Example 1: Assembly leaf function optimization**
```
optimize: sf_floor.s as leaf function (remove ra push/pop)

Problem:
sf_floor saved and restored return address (ra) unnecessarily, adding 2 instructions (~8 bytes) and extra memory traffic on every call.

Approach:
Eliminated ra push/pop by using tail-call pattern; function now only uses caller-saved registers and doesn't need to preserve ra.

Result:
Code size: 24 → 16 bytes (-33%)
No performance loss (leaf function overhead removed)
```

**Example 2: Register allocation tuning**
```
optimize: sinf.s improve register allocation for FPU operations

Problem:
Register spills were causing extra load/store operations in the hot path of sinf, adding latency on FPU-heavy workloads.

Approach:
Reordered argument reduction to keep intermediate values in registers longer; used callee-saved registers more strategically to reduce spill pressure.

Result:
Code size: 312 → 298 bytes (-4%)
Execution time: 142 → 136 cycles (-4%)
```

## What the Skill Does

- **Auto-detects function** from changed files (e.g., `sf_floor.s` → optimizing `sf_floor`)
- **Runs project benchmarks** to measure size/performance before/after
- **Extracts metrics** from benchmark output (code size, cycle count, etc.)
- **Allows customization** — you can provide problem and approach strings to override auto-detection

## When NOT to Use

This skill is **only** for optimization commits. Use regular git commit for:
- Bug fixes (unless they're performance-related)
- Refactoring without size/performance gains
- Documentation changes
- Adding new features

## Required Parameters (optional)

- `problem` — Brief explanation of what was suboptimal (auto-detected from function changes if omitted)
- `approach` — Description of the optimization technique (auto-detected from git diff if omitted)

The benchmark result is **always** captured automatically.

## Integration with Your Project

This skill is designed for Picolibc RISC-V libm work:
- Looks for Makefile `compare` target to run benchmarks
- Falls back to `commit-size` script if available
- Parses QEMU output or size tool output to extract metrics
- Works with both `-s` assembly files and C implementations in `hardfloat/`

input.command:
git add andes_fpexexp.s && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
optimize: andes_fpexexp.s remove 1 dead mul instruction

Problem:
In the polynomial loop (.Lb5), mulr64 $r0,$r7,$r0 was emitted as mulhu+mul
pair, but the low-half result (mul x10,x18,x10) was immediately overwritten
by mv x10,x11 which takes the high half. The mul destination was never read.

Approach:
Delete the dead mul. The mulhu result in x11 is still moved to x10 by the
subsequent mv, and the low half is not needed anywhere.

Result:
andes_fpexexp .text: 248 -> 244 bytes (-4 bytes, -1 instruction)
make test-expf test-coshf test-sinhf test-tanhf test-powf: all PASS

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

git add andes_dpexatan.s && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
optimize: andes_dpexatan.s remove 1 dead mul instruction

Problem:
In the slli/mul/mulhu/mv block at line 175, mulr64 $r6,$r7,$r3 was emitted as
mul+mulhu pair, but the low-half result (mul x9,x18,x13) was immediately
overwritten by mv x9,x18 which takes the high half. The mul destination
was never read.

Approach:
Delete the dead mul x9,x18,x13. The mulhu result in x18 is still moved into
x9 by the subsequent mv.

Result:
andes_dpexatan .text: 544 -> 540 bytes (-4 bytes, -1 instruction)
make test-atan test-asin test-acos: all PASS

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

git add andes_dpexsqrt.s && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
optimize: andes_dpexsqrt.s remove 2 dead mul instructions

Problem:
In both the odd-exponent (line 18) and even-exponent (line 32) arms, the
initial approximation step emitted mulr64 as mul+mulhu pairs. In each case
the low-half (mul x14,x11,x15) was immediately overwritten by mv x14,x21
which restores the saved x14. The mul destination was never read.

Approach:
Delete both dead mul instructions. The mulhu results (x15 = high half) are
still consumed by the subsequent add x15,x15,x21 constant correction.

Result:
andes_dpexsqrt .text: 522 -> 514 bytes (-8 bytes, -2 instructions)
make test-sqrt: PASS

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

git add andes_fpexatan.s && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
optimize: andes_fpexatan.s remove 5 dead mul instructions

Problem:
Five mulr64 emitted as mul+mulhu pairs had dead low-half results:
- Line 146: mul x14,x19,x10  - x14 overwritten at line 158 (sub x14,x17,x9)
- Line 213: mul x14,x10,x10  - x14 overwritten at line 226 (li x14,715472086)
- Line 224: mul x14,x19,x15  - x14 overwritten at line 226 (li x14,715472086)
- Line 228: mul x14,x19,x15  - x14 overwritten at line 230 (li x14,2147483826)
- Line 241: mul x10,x10,x15  - x10 overwritten at line 242 (mv x10,x11)
In every case only the mulhu high half was consumed; the mul low half was
not read before being clobbered.

Approach:
Delete all five dead mul instructions. Each mulhu is kept intact.

Result:
andes_fpexatan .text: 686 -> 666 bytes (-20 bytes, -5 instructions)
make test-atanf test-asinf test-acosf: all PASS

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
git add andes_fpexlog.s && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
optimize: andes_fpexlog.s remove dead mul, fold split prologue/epilogue

Problem:
Two independent inefficiencies:
1. In the polynomial loop (.Lb7), mulr64 $r2,$r3,$r2 emitted mul+mulhu, but
   the low-half result (mul x12,x13,x12) was immediately overwritten by
   addi x13,x16,0 then mv x12,x13. The mul destination was never read.
2. The outer frame used two separate stack adjustments (addi -16 for ra, then
   addi -32 for callee-saved registers) with matching split epilogue, costing
   one extra addi in each direction.

Approach:
1. Delete the dead mul x12,x13,x12. The mulhu result in x16 is kept.
2. Fold the two prologue addi pairs into a single addi sp,-48 and update
   the ra store/load offset from 0 to 32. Fold the two epilogue addi pairs
   into a single addi sp,+48.

Result:
andes_fpexlog .text: 664 -> 656 bytes (-8 bytes, net -3 instructions)
make test-logf test-log10f test-powf: all PASS

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.content:
diff --git a/libm/machine/riscv/plan.md b/libm/machine/riscv/plan.md
index 37817f403..36b40f8de 100644
--- a/libm/machine/riscv/plan.md
+++ b/libm/machine/riscv/plan.md
@@ -1,42 +1,132 @@
-# Pass B - Targeted Micro-Rewrites
-
-## Candidates
-
-### B1: `s_sqrt.s` - mulhu/mul operand swap (lines ~53-57 and ~66-70)
-- Pattern: `mulhu rA, x9, x15; mul rB, x9, x15; mv; mv; mv`
-- Fix: emit multiplies directly into final destination registers, drop the 3 `mv` instructions
-- Two occurrences; estimated savings: ~6 instructions (~12 bytes)
-- Status: DONE (854 -> 812 bytes, -42 bytes)
-
-### B2: `s_sqrt.s` - dead `mulhu` at line ~63
-- `mulhu x11, x9, x9` result is never read; x11 is unconditionally overwritten at line ~66
-- Fix: delete the dead instruction
-- Estimated savings: 1 instruction (~2-4 bytes)
-- Status: DONE (812 -> 808 bytes, -4 bytes)
-
-### B3: `s_fmod.s` - hoist duplicate `li 2147483648` (lines ~22 and ~30)
-- The constant `0x80000000` is loaded twice into two different scratch registers
-- Each large `li` assembles to `lui + addi` = 8 bytes
-- Fix: load once, reuse; reorder surrounding `slli` ops so the register is not clobbered
-- Estimated savings: 1 large `li` (~8 bytes)
-- Status: DONE (700 -> 696 bytes, -4 bytes)
-
-### B4: `s_log1p.s` - `li 0` -> `sw x0` (lines ~110-113)
-- `li x10, 0; li x11, 0; sw x10, N(sp); sw x11, M(sp)` can become `sw x0, N(sp); sw x0, M(sp)`
